name: United States (1877–1890)
description: Thirteen alternating red and white horizontal stripes (seven red, six white). In the upper hoist, a blue canton bearing 38 white five-pointed stars in five rows of 7-8-8-8-7.
history: Adopted on 4 July 1877 following the admission of Colorado as the 38th state on 1 August 1876. It served as the official flag for thirteen years until 4 July 1890, when the 43-star flag was introduced. As star arrangements were not regulated before 1912, many other patterns of this flag also circulated.

features.symbolism: the union of the states; a new constellation
the 38 states of the Union as of 4 July 1877
